methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: A user's unlock time must be monotonically increasing and cannot be reduced.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker can bypass the lock duration by calling setLockDuration with a specific value. Because the new unlock time is calculated as lastLockTime + _duration, and the check only verifies block.timestamp + _duration >= current unlockTime, a user can significantly reduce their unlockTime (even to the current block.timestamp) if lastLockTime was in the past, allowing premature withdrawal of locked tokens and theft of time-locked rewards.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_unlockTimeMonotonicity(address user, address token) public view { assert(lockedTokens[user][token].unlockTime >= previousUnlockTime[user][token]); }

invariants.description: A user's remainder (tokens not yet converted to NFTs) must never exceed their total locked quantity.
invariants.attack_scenario: When a user unlocks tokens, their locked quantity decreases but their remainder is not updated or cleared. A user can unlock all their tokens (quantity = 0) while keeping a non-zero remainder. They can then lock a small amount of tokens to combine with this phantom remainder to mint NFTs at a discount, extracting protocol value without providing the required collateral.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_remainderBoundedByQuantity(address user, address token) public view { assert(lockedTokens[user][token].remainder <= lockedTokens[user][token].quantity); }

invariants.description: The contract's actual token balance must be at least the sum of all users' locked quantities.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a fee-on-transfer token is configured, the contract receives fewer tokens than the _quantity added to the user's locked balance. This breaks the 1:1 backing of locked tokens, leading to insolvency where the last users to unlock will be unable to withdraw their principal.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_solvencyOfLockedTokens(address token) public view { assert(IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(this)) >= ghost_totalLockedQuantity[token]); }
